claims.notes: Bootstrap coordination guardrail for parallel Codex/Claude work. This is coordination evidence, not approval or authority.
Stacked on PR #160 because mini-crawler depends on mini-web-types. Deterministic crawler planning/admission only; no network fetcher or parser.

Fix Windows compile failure: gate std::os::unix::fs::symlink behind #[cfg(unix)], add runtime-Unsupported fallback on other platforms. No behavior change on Unix; no new capability. Closed independently by both the issue #190 and issue #192 branches after PR #171 was confirmed merged; found stale (still in_review) and closed per the standing precedent of closing merged-but-unclosed claims on the next PR that touches this file (see issue #186's own closing note).

Track B3: extractor protocol (mini-extract-protocol) + isolated host (mini-extract-host), mirroring mini-pipeline-protocol/mini-build-runner-wasmtime's subprocess-isolation discipline. One simple plain-text-normalize extractor; PDF/HTML deferred to Track B4. Closed here (issue #182 branch) since PR #172 merged 2026-07-19T20:06:58Z, the same precedent PR #174 set for #161.

CI scope only. Narrows the reproducibility job's build to the four crates whose example binaries it already hashes; the two-clean-build discipline and the compared artifact set are unchanged. Also closes the stale claim for issue #161, whose PR #162 merged 2026-07-19T01:22:31Z. Closed here (issue #182 branch) since PR #174 itself merged, the same precedent PR #174 set for #161 and PR #180 set for #173/#177.

Phase 2 of post-quantum identity migration (D-0095, issue #15): ML-DSA-65 key generation + isolated signing in mini-crypto. No did-mini/KEL wiring, no default-suite change. Decision renumbered D-0320 -> D-0322 during rebase to avoid colliding with PR #174's independently-claimed D-0320 (CI reproducibility scope) and PR #180's D-0321 (KEL witness receipt types). Closed independently by both the issue #190 and issue #192 branches after PR #176 was confirmed merged; found stale (still in_review) and closed per the same standing precedent as issue #167's closure above.

Phase 1 of KEL witness receipts (audit #12 F4, invariant M3): WitnessPolicy/WitnessReceiptStatement/WitnessReceipt/WitnessedEventCertificate types in did-mini. Canonical encoding + signature/threshold verification only, no witness state machine, no KEL-verification wiring, no network. Closed here (issue #186 branch) since PR #180 merged 2026-07-20T06:53Z.

Fix an intermittent CI failure (most recently blocking PR #181's required check job): a_zero_millisecond_deadline_is_reported_as_timeout raced Duration::ZERO against the worker's real round trip via mpsc::Receiver::recv_timeout. Special-cased max_wall_clock_ms == 0 in run_worker to a deterministic immediate timeout. No behavior change for non-zero deadlines. Closed here (issue #184 branch) since PR #183 merged 2026-07-20T09:38Z.

Fix the reproducibility workflow's required-check gap: paths-ignore for docs/**/**/*.md meant the job never ran (and the required status check never posted a conclusion) for a PR whose entire diff was docs-only, leaving it permanently mergeable_state: blocked (observed on PR #181). Workflow now always triggers; the two-clean-build cost is skipped inside the job via a real git-diff check instead. Closed here (issue #186 branch) since PR #185 merged.

Phase 2 of KEL witness receipts (audit #12 F4, invariant M3): WitnessJournal in-memory witness state machine (first-seen acceptance, direct-successor verification, duplicate idempotence, stale rejection, same-sequence conflict detection) plus ControllerDuplicityProof and WitnessEquivocationProof. No KEL-chain verification wiring (Phase 3), no fork-proof construction for conflicting descendants, no gossip, no persistence. Closed here (issue #188 branch) since PR #187 merged.

First slice of Batch 5's 'local object indexing at scale': a chronological idx/time/ index plus Store::since/Store::recent query methods. Still O(matching-prefix-subtree) like every other Store index, not yet a bounded/paginated range scan -- that needs a new Backend range-query primitive, explicit follow-up. Closed independently by both the issue #190 and issue #192 branches after PR #189 merged.

Three related slices of Phase 3 of KEL witness receipts (audit #12 F4, invariant M3), batched into one PR per CLAUDE.md's workflow ritual since each built directly on the last in the same session before the branch was ever pushed. D-0328: did_mini::assess_kel_assurance classifies Direct/Pinned/Witnessed/WitnessedRecent/DuplicityDetected by composing Kel::verify (via FreshnessPins) with a caller-supplied WitnessedEventCertificate/WitnessPolicy and known_duplicity flag. D-0329: WitnessJournal::observe_verified runs the real Kel::verify chain over the entire presented KEL before delegating to observe, closing the 'trusts the caller' gap observe's own doc comment named. D-0330: DuplicityRegistry records ControllerDuplicityProof/WitnessEquivocationProof and answers has_known_duplicity(identity, policy), a real place to compute known_duplicity from instead of by hand. Still open: WitnessPolicy read from a real Establishment event, persistence/gossip for the registry, a bounded/incremental re-verify, and any real call site gating an authority decision or feeding real proofs into the registry. Closed independently by both the issue #192 and issue #194 branches after PR #191 was confirmed merged.

Follow-up to D-0327: Backend::list_meta_prefix_last, a genuinely bounded 'most recent N' query. MemoryBackend gets a real O(log n + limit) BTreeMap::range(...).rev().take(limit) implementation; FsBackend inherits the trait's non-bounded default (deliberately -- a real bounded FS reverse walk needs either a sorted early-stopping traversal or an on-disk sorted index, not attempted here to avoid risking FsBackend's existing symlink/path-traversal defenses). Store::recent now calls the new method directly instead of since(0) reversed/truncated client-side. Store::since's own forward scan is unchanged and still not bounded. Closed here (PR #179 branch, during its rebase past main) since PR #193 was confirmed merged; found stale (still in_review) and closed per the standing precedent of closing merged-but-unclosed claims on the next PR that touches this file.

mini_forge::author_assurance composes the oracle's existing author_verified provenance re-check with did_mini::assess_kel_assurance over the author-root's KEL -- the first consumer of KelAssurance outside did-mini itself. Deliberately not wired into propose/approve/merge/resolve_project's actual quorum gating (still purely author_verified's boolean) -- which governance action should require which minimum assurance level is a founder-facing policy call, not decided unilaterally here. Closed here (issue #207 branch) since PR #195 was confirmed merged (main's own merge commit); found stale (still in_review) and closed per the standing precedent of closing merged-but-unclosed claims on the next PR that touches this file.

BountyPool::project label + with_project builder, purely organizational metadata for grouping multiple flat-amount funding rounds under one project name. No change to claim/verify/ring logic. Closed here (PR #179 branch, during its rebase past main) since PR #208 was confirmed merged (main's own merge commit).

Android-first foundation: a narrow Rust UniFFI command/event boundary and thin Kotlin/Compose shell. The first identity/profile/follow integration slice depends on the verified social delivery work in draft PR #170. Coordination evidence only; no approval, release, or production claim. Rebased past main through PR #180/#183/#185/#187/#189/#191/#193/#195/#208 (see this PR's own body for what changed).
